mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate and signals policy support for risk assets, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days after rate cuts become visible in the data.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and earnings expectations, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO accelerates while PAYEMS is flat or falling, firms are producing more with less labor, which supports margins and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term INDPRO change minus PAYEMS change has no positive sign relation with 5-day forward returns, or if single-series effects explain the signal equally well without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A break above the prior trend in RSAFS indicates stronger retail demand and supports near-term revenue expectations, which should push S&P 500 returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if months with RSAFS above its 6-month moving average do not show higher 20-day forward S&P 500 returns than months below it, or if the sign flips in more than one major rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation running above the federal funds rate implies negative real policy rates, which is supportive for equities through easier financial conditions and higher present values of future earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 1 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods do not outperform untriggered periods on 20-day returns, or if higher inflation relative to FEDFUNDS is followed by negative rather than positive equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and construction activity, which usually precedes slower broad demand growth and lower S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if a 3-month decline in HOUST is not followed by below-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the signal loses predictive power after accounting for trend persistence.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, payroll loss and rising joblessness jointly confirm labor-market deterioration, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-down and UNRATE-up condition does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered observations have positive 20-day returns more often than negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad real demand repair, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations revise upward.
failure_condition: Reject if paired improvements in GDP and retail sales do not coincide with higher 60-day forward returns than the unconditional average, or if the joint signal is not stronger than either proxy separately.
